data.title: IMAX reports Q3 earnings: EPS of 47 cents (beat estimate of 38 cents), sales $106.65 million, content solutions revenue $44.8 million, gross box office $367.6 million (50% YoY increase), accounting for 4.2% of global box office (49% YoY increase).
data.description: IMAX Corp (NYSE: IMAX) stock rose after reporting Q3 results. The company's adjusted EPS of 47 cents exceeded the analyst estimate of 38 cents. Quarterly sales were $106.65 million, up 17% from last year and beating the estimate of $105.16 million. Content Solutions revenues grew by 49% to $44.8 million. IMAX's gross box office rose 50% to $367.6 million, setting a new Q3 record. IMAX accounted for 4.2% of the global box office, up 49% from last year. The success was attributed to a diverse film mix, including the anime hit "Demon Slayer: Infinity Castle".

data.title: Inventor develops DeerChaser.com to repel deer, combat garden destruction. Patent-pending novelty aims to deter invasive hungry predators.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on October 23, 2025, a homeowner shared their struggle with deer damaging their flowerbeds and plants, leading to the creation of DeerChaser.com. This innovative solution aims to deter persistent deer herds. The homeowner developed a patent-pending technology to address the issue. The platform offers a fun and effective way to protect foliage.

data.title: Metrika selected for 'Ones to Watch' list in Chartis RiskTech100® 2026 for Blockchain Risk Management.
data.description: In a recent report released on October 23, 2025, Metrika has been acknowledged in the first-ever 'Digital Asset Data and Risk Management' section, showcasing the increasing emphasis of the traditional financial sector on Blockchain risk oversight. The Chartis RiskTech100 2026 rankings highlighted Metrika as a standout in this category. This recognition underlines the pivotal role played by Metrika amidst the changing landscape of financial technology, reflecting the industry's shifting focus towards robust risk management in the Blockchain sphere.

data.title: Ascendion named global leader in ISG Provider Lens for Generative AI Services 2025, AAVA™ platform driving enterprise impact.
data.description: Ascendion has been recognized as a global leader by ISG, ranking among the top companies worldwide in Generative AI strategy and delivery. Their Agentic AI platform, AAVA™, is instrumental in translating vision into tangible business outcomes, showcasing their capability to turn strategy into measurable impact on an enterprise scale. Ascendion emphasizes the synergy between humans and AI agents to enhance productivity and efficiency, showcasing their commitment to collaborative and innovative solutions in the industry.

data.title: General Micro Systems won 4 'Best in Show' awards at AUSA 2025 for their X7 RAPTOR™ product.
data.description: General Micro Systems, based in Rancho Cucamonga, California, received four 4-star Best in Show Awards from Military Embedded Systems at the recent Association for the United States Army (AUSA) Annual Meeting & Exposition in Washington, D.C. The awards were given to their new X7 RAPTOR™ product, recognized for its innovation and mission-advancing capabilities. This technology-independent supplier of computing engines in boxes, boards, and servers showcased the exceptionally low-cost and lightweight X7 RAPTOR™, impressing attendees at the event.

data.title: Kinetic report shows 22% confident in digital security, 77% worry about internet safety, most concerned about large data breaches and storing personal data outside U.S.
data.description: A recent survey by Kinetic, a fiber internet provider, revealed concerning statistics regarding digital safety and privacy. Only 22% of respondents feel confident in their digital security measures, while 77% express concerns about their internet safety. 60% feel unsafe online due to the current global climate and the rise of artificial intelligence. The survey, which included 436 respondents across 18 states, identified top concerns as large data breaches (72%), storing personal data outside the U.S. (69%), targeted advertising (64%), AI (61%), and economic uncertainty (51%). The report highlights a growing worry among consumers about their online security and privacy, with two-thirds being more cautious with their personal data.

data.title: 86% of companies are fast-tracking global hiring due to wage inflation and AI talent shortage, with 48% expecting international workers to make up half their workforce by 2027. Global hiring forecasted to jump 57% in the next year, reaching 69% in 24 months. U.S leads with 46% ramping up hiring, followed by Germany (43%) and U.K. (38%).
data.description: A new survey by Pebl shows 86% of HR and finance leaders planning to expand hiring abroad within two years, with 48% expecting international workers to account for 50% or more of their workforce by 2027. 87% of leaders believe hiring abroad is economically beneficial due to wage inflation and AI talent shortages. CEO Francoise Brougher states that companies neglecting global hiring will lag behind. Global hiring is accelerating, with a projected 57% increase in international hiring in the next year and 69% within 24 months. The U.S. is leading this trend, followed by Germany and the U.K. The U.K. plans to double global hiring, surpassing the U.S. and Germany in this initiative.

data.title: Tiger Pistol releases 2026 Planning Guides on local advertising, focusing on AI and automation for growth.
data.description: Tiger Pistol has just unveiled its 2026 Planning Guides during an event in Cleveland on October 23, 2025. These guides provide insight into how AI and automation are reshaping the landscape of local growth for franchises, multi-location brands, QSRs, and marketing resellers. The roadmaps offer a glimpse into the future, highlighting the significant impact these technologies will have on the industry. This release marks an important step for businesses looking to stay ahead of the curve, with Tiger Pistol leading the way in providing crucial information for strategic planning in the coming year.

data.title: PAVE awarded by Deloitte's Technology Fast 50 for rapid growth & innovation in AI vehicle inspection. Top 50 in Canada.
data.description: The company PAVE, which specializes in AI-driven vehicle inspection technology, has recently been honored by Deloitte's 2025 Technology Fast 50 awards program for its remarkable expansion, innovative approach, and entrepreneurial drive. This prestigious recognition highlights PAVE's impressive growth within Canada's fastest-growing businesses, acknowledging its pioneering advancements in the industry.

data.title: Alcohol Justice highlights breast cancer-alcohol link during Breast Cancer Awareness Month, raising awareness on low public recognition.
data.description: In San Rafael, California, on October 23, 2025, despite the diligent efforts of advocates and extensive research, public awareness about breast cancer remains alarmingly low. As October marks Breast Cancer Awareness Month, Alcohol Justice acknowledges the dedication of researchers and advocates in raising awareness. Their commitment signifies a crucial need for national recognition of this issue.

data.title: DTEN introduces D7X 27: All-in-One Zoom Rooms Appliance for various workspaces. valid Oct 23, 2025.
data.description: In San Jose, California on October 23, 2025, DTEN unveiled the DTEN D7X 27, a new collaboration device designed for huddle rooms, kiosks, and frontline workspaces. This sleek and compact technology aims to enhance video conferencing and collaborative efforts. The device is optimized for smaller meeting spaces and work environments, catering to the growing demand for efficient communication tools. This release follows DTEN's success in the industry and further solidifies its position as a global leader in this sector.

data.title: Built In introduced a platform to enhance employer reputation in AI hiring market, aiding in attract talent.
data.description: On October 23, 2025, in Chicago, the recruitment and employer reputation platform, Built In, introduced a groundbreaking solution to assist companies in evaluating and enhancing their employer brand reputation in AI search. This innovative tool aims to improve companies' visibility and trustworthiness in the field, ensuring they stand out in the competitive landscape.

data.title: UNI price set to hit $7.50 by Nov 2025, with 20% potential gain in 4-6 weeks amid positive technical momentum. Read more.
data.description: The price prediction for UNI indicates a positive trend, with a medium-term target of $7.50. The current technical analysis suggests a potential 20% increase in value over the next 4-6 weeks.

data.title: Siteimprove's Tobias Nyhuus Jensen named Denmark's expert in AccessibleEU Initiative, demonstrating commitment to digital accessibility.
data.description: Siteimprove, a company based in Copenhagen, Denmark, has reaffirmed its dedication to promoting global digital accessibility standards. On October 23, 2025, the company's leader Tobias Nyhuus Jensen shared this commitment as they continue to lead in agentic content intelligence. This announcement solidifies Siteimprove's position as a key player in advancing accessibility with a strong focus on innovative solutions.

data.title: Fort Bragg's single soldiers rate housing high, with Corvias earning Platinum A-List for Randolph Pointe Apartments.
data.description: At Fort Bragg, unaccompanied and single soldiers are expressing high satisfaction levels with Army housing, citing the quality and modern design of current apartment and single-unit options. Corvias, a reliable infrastructure and resiliency partner to the military and higher education, received the Platinum A-List Award for its Randolph Pointe Apartments. This recognition comes as service quality, maintenance responsiveness, and overall living experiences reach five-year highs. The soldiers at Fort Bragg highlighted these aspects, showcasing the success of the housing provided, resulting in a positive endorsement of the housing conditions.

data.title: Symphony Talent wins 4 awards at 2025 Recruitment Marketing Awards, including Campaign of the Year and Grand Prix with client bp.
data.description: On October 23, 2025, Symphony Talent, a renowned talent acquisition solutions provider, celebrated their achievements at the 2025 Recruitment Marketing Awards (RMAs). They secured a total of four awards in partnership with their client, bp, a notable energy company. Notably, they clinched prestigious titles such as Campaign of the Year and Grand Prix.
